title: eryngium aquaticum
intro: Eryngium aquaticum, also known as sea holly or sea blue devil, is a perennial plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its spiky blue or white flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Eryngium aquaticum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, Eryngium aquaticum is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
